Pelluco, spa and tourist town in the commune of Puerto Montt. **2nd row:** Night panoramic view of downtown Puerto. **3rd row:** Monument to Vicente Pérez Rosales, and eruption of the Calbuco volcano from the city. **4th row:** On the left, Bell Tower of the Society of Jesus; on the right-top, Church of the Jesuit Fathers; to the right-below, Chinquihue stadium. | |

([Mapuche](/wiki/Mapuche "Mapuche"): *Meli Pulli*) is a port [city](/wiki/List_of_cities_in_Chile "List of cities in Chile") and [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile") in southern [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"), located at the northern end of the [Reloncaví Sound](/wiki/Reloncav%C3%AD_Sound "Reloncaví Sound") in the [Llanquihue Province](/wiki/Llanquihue_Province "Llanquihue Province"), [Los Lagos Region](/wiki/Los_Lagos_Region "Los Lagos Region"), 1,055 km to the south of the capital, [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago,_Chile "Santiago, Chile"). The commune spans an area of 1,673 km2 (646 sq mi) and has a population of 245,902 in 2017. It is bounded by the communes of [Puerto Varas](/wiki/Puerto_Varas "Puerto Varas") to the north, [Cochamó](/wiki/Cocham%C3%B3 "Cochamó") to the east and southeast, [Calbuco](/wiki/Calbuco "Calbuco") to the southwest and [Maullín](/wiki/Maull%C3%ADn "Maullín") and [Los Muermos](/wiki/Los_Muermos "Los Muermos") to the west.

**Puerto Montt** ([Mapuche](/wiki/Mapuche "Mapuche"): *Meli Pulli*) is a port [city](/wiki/List_of_cities_in_Chile "List of cities in Chile") and [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile") in southern [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"), located at the northern end of the [Reloncaví Sound](/wiki/Reloncav%C3%AD_Sound "Reloncaví Sound") in the [Llanquihue Province](/wiki/Llanquihue_Province "Llanquihue Province"), [Los Lagos Region](/wiki/Los_Lagos_Region "Los Lagos Region"), 1,055 km to the south of the capital, [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago,_Chile "Santiago, Chile"). The commune spans an area of 1,673 km2 (646 sq mi) and has a population of 245,902 in 2017. It is bounded by the communes of [Puerto Varas](/wiki/Puerto_Varas "Puerto Varas") to the north, [Cochamó](/wiki/Cocham%C3%B3 "Cochamó") to the east and southeast, [Calbuco](/wiki/Calbuco "Calbuco") to the southwest and [Maullín](/wiki/Maull%C3%ADn "Maullín") and [Los Muermos](/wiki/Los_Muermos "Los Muermos") to the west.

Founded as late as 1853 during the [German colonization of southern Chile](/wiki/German_colonization_of_Valdivia,_Osorno_and_Llanquihue "German colonization of Valdivia, Osorno and Llanquihue"), Puerto Montt soon outgrew older neighboring cities through its strategic position at the southern end of the [Chilean Central Valley](/wiki/Chilean_Central_Valley "Chilean Central Valley") being a gateway city into the [Chiloé Archipelago](/wiki/Chilo%C3%A9_Archipelago "Chiloé Archipelago"), the [Llanquihue](/wiki/Llanquihue_Lake "Llanquihue Lake") and [Nahuel Huapi](/wiki/Nahuel_Huapi_Lake "Nahuel Huapi Lake") lakes and [Western Patagonia](/wiki/Zona_Austral "Zona Austral").

Puerto Montt has gained renown and grown significantly through the rise of Chile to become the second largest salmon producer of the world during the 1990s and 2000s. However, the [Chilean salmon aquaculture crisis](/wiki/Aquaculture_in_Chile "Aquaculture in Chile") of the late 2000s resulted in severe [unemployment](/wiki/Unemployment "Unemployment") and exposed weaknesses in the local economy. The city's cultural endowment mixes elements of Chilean-Spanish culture with a German heritage. The city has attracted a significant number of newcomers from all over Chile in the last 30 years[*[which?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Avoid_weasel_words "Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words")*] in search of employment opportunities.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Originally, the site was covered by thick forest and was called *Melipulli* (which means "four hills" in [Mapudungun](/wiki/Mapudungun "Mapudungun")). It was selected as an entrance to [Lake Llanquihue](/wiki/Lake_Llanquihue "Lake Llanquihue") when its proximity to the open sea was discovered by the colonial government. The expedition was entrusted to [Bernardo Philippi](/wiki/Bernhard_Eunom_Philippi "Bernhard Eunom Philippi"), a German naturalist and cartographer, but after his death in 1851, Vicente Perez Rosales took over his duties, and by the end of September started to chop trees at Reloncaví sound using local woodsman coming from Huar, Maillen, Huelmo and Calbuco Mainly. By December, after the forest was cut down, the area was burned to clear the land in anticipation of completing the settlement plan.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The city itself was founded on February 12, 1853, after government-sponsored [immigration from Germany](/wiki/German_colonization_of_Valdivia,_Osorno_and_Llanquihue "German colonization of Valdivia, Osorno and Llanquihue") that began in 1848 populated the region and integrated it politically to the rest of the country.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It was named after [Manuel Montt](/wiki/Manuel_Montt "Manuel Montt"), [President of Chile](/wiki/President_of_Chile "President of Chile") between 1851 and 1861, who set in motion the German immigration.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1912, the city was connected by train to Santiago, making it an important point of entry into Chilean Patagonia and augmenting its commercial development. By 1950, it had a population of 27,500, and the city was rapidly urbanizing. However, the [1960 Valdivia earthquake](/wiki/1960_Valdivia_earthquake "1960 Valdivia earthquake") destroyed much of Puerto Montt, collapsing the port and the train station along with many building and houses. Eventually the city recovered, becoming once again an important urban centre as well as a port of national interest.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### 1969 violence

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 1969 violence")]

Main article: [Massacre of Puerto Montt](/wiki/Massacre_of_Puerto_Montt "Massacre of Puerto Montt")

At the start of March 1969, approximately 90 landless people received poor advice from [Socialist](/wiki/Socialist_Party_of_Chile "Socialist Party of Chile") [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_of_Chile "Chamber of Deputies of Chile") Luis Espinoza.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The families were never granted land needed to build their homes. On March 4, 1969, the families settled on empty, unoccupied farmland owned by an absentee landlord.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The families sought squatters' rights. Approximately four to five days after moving onto the land, the local [Police Chief](/wiki/Carabineros_de_Chile "Carabineros de Chile") Rolando Rodríguez Marbán assured the squatters that they would not be disturbed, and could proceed with their home construction. Unfortunately, the Ministry of the Interior changed their orders, which led to tragic results.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On midnight on March 9, Espinoza was charged with breaking the law, arrested, and moved to the city of [Valdivia](/wiki/Valdivia_(city) "Valdivia (city)"), where he was held. Following direct orders from the Minister of Interior [Edmundo Pérez Zujovic](/wiki/Edmundo_P%C3%A9rez_Zujovic "Edmundo Pérez Zujovic"), 250 armed policemen launched an assault on the squatting families at dawn. Eight squatters were shot dead. Two squatters, a 9-month child and an adult later died from their wounds. The newly built homes were decimated.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The massacre is poignantly memorialized by singer-songwriter [Víctor Jara](/wiki/V%C3%ADctor_Jara "Víctor Jara") in his song [Preguntas por Puerto Montt](/wiki/Pongo_En_Tus_Manos_Abiertas_(album)#Preguntas_por_Puerto_Montt "Pongo En Tus Manos Abiertas (album)").

### Late 20th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Late 20th century")]

By 1979, Puerto Montt was nominated and later become the Capital of Los Lagos Region, thereby becoming the principal administrative, political and commercial centre of Southern Chile.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

By 1982, the commune already reached 103,680 inhabitants. From the late 1980s, the city began to experience a new accelerated growth, both in population and in the economic sphere, mainly due to the installation of the industry Chilean salmon farm, of which Puerto Montt became its nerve center.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Climate")]

Puerto Montt has a wet [oceanic climate](/wiki/Oceanic_climate "Oceanic climate") ([Köppen](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"): *Cfb*) with heavy rainfall throughout the year but a drying trend in the summer. Although temperatures are consistently below 25 °C (77 °F), frosts are very rare and occur only a couple of times per month in the winter.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Economy")]

See also: [Aquaculture in Chile](/wiki/Aquaculture_in_Chile "Aquaculture in Chile")

Puerto Montt is the capital of the [Los Lagos Region](/wiki/Los_Lagos_Region "Los Lagos Region") and the [Llanquihue Province](/wiki/Llanquihue_Province "Llanquihue Province"), and the main sea port at the lower end of Chile's western continental land. The city is the principal commercial, services, and financial hub of the Chilean [Northern Patagonia](/wiki/Patagonia#Chilean_Patagonia "Patagonia")—[Zona Austral](/wiki/Zona_Austral "Zona Austral").

Puerto Montt is also the gateway to the [Chiloé Archipelago](/wiki/Chilo%C3%A9_Archipelago "Chiloé Archipelago") and [Chiloé Island](/wiki/Chilo%C3%A9_Island "Chiloé Island") across the [Chacao Channel](/wiki/Chacao_Channel "Chacao Channel"), and the many other smaller islands in Chile's inland [Sea of Chiloé](/wiki/Sea_of_Chilo%C3%A9 "Sea of Chiloé").

The city's economy is now based upon agriculture, cattle, and [forestry](/wiki/Forestry "Forestry") on the surrounding islands, and fishing and [salmon aquaculture](/wiki/Aquaculture_of_salmon "Aquaculture of salmon") in the [fjords](/wiki/Fjords_and_channels_of_Chile "Fjords and channels of Chile") and nearby Pacific Ocean.

### History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: History")]

During the 19th century, before and after the founding of Puerto Montt, the extraction and commercialization of wood, mainly larch, was the main economic activity in the area. Other existing items since the creation of the city were, to a lesser extent, alcohol distilleries, grain oil factories, breweries and the sale of items imported from Germany or Valparaíso. Puerto Montt, as the capital, was vital in the entry and exit of merchandise to the area, since the seaway was the only way to get the products out - the train would only arrive in 1912.

Salmon aquaculture

The city is configured as the hub of one of the largest [salmon aquaculture](/wiki/Aquaculture_of_salmon "Aquaculture of salmon") industries in the world. Hatcheries, fisheries and packing plants are mostly located south of Puerto Montt. Fresh salmon is flown daily to world markets and frozen salmon is shipped by ocean to all destinations. The tremendous growth of the region, mainly due to the salmon industry, but also due to rapid expansion of forestry, cattle, and tourism, has proven the massive economic potential of Puerto Montt and its surrounding area.

## International relations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: International relations")]

The city of Puerto Montt hosts a number of international relations institutions, such as the Regional Unit of International Affairs (URAI) of the [Regional Government of Los Lagos](/w/index.php?title=Regional_Government_of_Los_Lagos&action=edit&redlink=1 "Regional Government of Los Lagos (page does not exist)"), responsible for analyzing and managing the region’s bilateral and multilateral relations with [Latin America](/wiki/Latin_America "Latin America") and the rest of the world; the Tourism and International Relations Commission of the Regional Council of Los Lagos; the regional office of the [National Migration Service](/w/index.php?title=National_Migration_Service&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Migration Service (page does not exist)"); the regional office of the [General Directorate for Export Promotion](/w/index.php?title=General_Directorate_for_Export_Promotion&action=edit&redlink=1 "General Directorate for Export Promotion (page does not exist)") (ProChile); the Department of Migration and International Police of the [Investigations Police](/wiki/Investigations_Police_of_Chile "Investigations Police of Chile"); the Migrant Office,[[15]](#cite_note-15) and the International Relations Officer of the Municipality of Puerto Montt.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In the field of higher education internationalization, the main actor in Puerto Montt is the Directorate of International Relations of the [University of Los Lagos](/wiki/University_of_Los_Lagos "University of Los Lagos").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Transport")]

[El Tepual Airport](/wiki/El_Tepual_Airport "El Tepual Airport") is the main commercial airport serving the city. Three airlines operate regular flights to and from [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago,_Chile "Santiago, Chile"), [Punta Arenas](/wiki/Punta_Arenas "Punta Arenas"), [Balmaceda](/wiki/Balmaceda,_Chile "Balmaceda, Chile"), and [Antofagasta](/wiki/Antofagasta,_Chile "Antofagasta, Chile"). The airlines operating at the airport are [LATAM Airlines](/wiki/LATAM_Airlines "LATAM Airlines") (the country's largest), [Sky Airline](/wiki/Sky_Airline "Sky Airline") and [JetSmart](/wiki/JetSmart "JetSmart"). The flight time to Santiago is approximately two hours.

Additionally, [Marcel Marchant Aerodrome (La Paloma)](/wiki/Marcel_Marchant_Airport "Marcel Marchant Airport") connects the city with remote locations within the Patagonian Fjords.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Train services to the city by [Empresa de los Ferrocarriles del Estado](/wiki/Empresa_de_los_Ferrocarriles_del_Estado "Empresa de los Ferrocarriles del Estado"), after a long absence since the 2000s, returned in 2025 with a local service from [Llanquihue](/wiki/Llanquihue,_Chile "Llanquihue, Chile") to the city's [La Paloma Station](/wiki/Puerto_Montt_-_La_Paloma_Train_Station "Puerto Montt - La Paloma Train Station"), with plans to reopen stations to the north in the coming years.

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Administration")]

As a commune, Puerto Montt is a third-level [administrative division of Chile](/wiki/Administrative_division_of_Chile "Administrative division of Chile") administered by a [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council "Municipal council"), headed by an [Alcalde](/wiki/Alcalde "Alcalde") with 10 councilors and represented in the [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_of_Chile "Chamber of Deputies of Chile") by 5 members and in the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_of_Chile "Senate of Chile") by 2 people and with support of a Regional Council of 6 members.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

The 2024–2028 mayor is [Rodrigo Wainraihgt Galilea](/wiki/Rodrigo_Wainraihgt "Rodrigo Wainraihgt") ([RN](/wiki/National_Renewal_(Chile) "National Renewal (Chile)")). The current members of the council are:[[19]](#cite_note-19)

* José Segura Díaz (RN)
* Verónica Cárdenas Navarro (RN)
* Yerco Rodríguez Guichapani ([UDI](/wiki/Independent_Democratic_Union "Independent Democratic Union"))
* Fernando Brinder Álvarez (Ind./UDI)
* Mirta Vega Barría ([REP](/wiki/Republican_Party_(Chile,_2019) "Republican Party (Chile, 2019)"))
* Montserrat Muller Sanhueza (REP)
* Barbara Álvarez Mendez ([PPD](/wiki/Party_for_Democracy_(Chile) "Party for Democracy (Chile)"))
* Barbara Cáceres Martínez ([PL](/wiki/Liberal_Party_of_Chile_(2013) "Liberal Party of Chile (2013)"))
* Sebastián Almonacid Fuica ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_of_Chile "Socialist Party of Chile"))
* Evelyn Chávez Chávez ([FA](/wiki/Broad_Front_(Chilean_political_party) "Broad Front (Chilean political party)"))

Within the [electoral division of Chile](/wiki/Electoral_division_of_Chile "Electoral division of Chile"), representatives of Puerto Montt, District 26 (Puerto Montt, [Cochamó](/wiki/Cocham%C3%B3 "Cochamó"), [Maullín](/wiki/Maull%C3%ADn "Maullín"), [Calbuco](/wiki/Calbuco "Calbuco"), [Castro](/wiki/Castro,_Chile "Castro, Chile"), [Ancud](/wiki/Ancud "Ancud"), [Quemchi](/wiki/Quemchi "Quemchi"), [Dalcahue](/wiki/Dalcahue "Dalcahue"), [Curaco de Vélez](/wiki/Curaco_de_V%C3%A9lez "Curaco de Vélez"), [Quinchao](/wiki/Quinchao "Quinchao"), [Puqueldón](/wiki/Puqueld%C3%B3n "Puqueldón"), [Chonchi](/wiki/Chonchi "Chonchi"), [Queilén](/wiki/Queil%C3%A9n "Queilén"), [Quellón](/wiki/Quell%C3%B3n "Quellón"), [Chaitén](/wiki/Chait%C3%A9n "Chaitén"), [Hualaihué](/wiki/Hualaihu%C3%A9 "Hualaihué"), [Futaleufú](/wiki/Futaleuf%C3%BA,_Chile "Futaleufú, Chile") and [Palena](/wiki/Palena,_Chile "Palena, Chile")) in the Chambers of Deputies 2018-2022 are Jenny Álvarez Vera (PS), Gabriel Ascencio Mansilla (PDC), Alejandro Santana Tirachini (RN), Carlos Kuschel Silva (RN) and Alejandro Bernales ([Liberal Party](/wiki/Liberal_Party_of_Chile "Liberal Party of Chile")).
The current Representatives in the Senate period 2014-2022 are Rabindranath Quinteros Lara (PS) and Ivan Moreira Barros ([UDI](/wiki/Independent_Democratic_Union "Independent Democratic Union")).
Regional councilors members 2018-2022 are Ricardo Kuschel Silva (RN), Jaime Brahm Barril (RN), Juan Ortiz Roble (UDI), Manuel Rivera Altamirano (PS), Juan Carcamo Carcamo (PDC) and Valentina Alvarez (Ind/PPD).[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Puerto_Montt&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Education")]

Puerto Montt has 175 registered schools varying from [educational levels](/wiki/Education_in_Chile "Education in Chile") such as pre-school, primary school, secondary school, special school as well as technical-professional and adult schools.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

* Arabe Siria School is a Catholic, public school teaching from pre-school to primary school.
* Alerce Rural School is a Catholic, public school from pre-school to primary school with some support to children with special needs.
* Angelmo Elementary School is a public school with education levels from pre-school to primary school with support to children with special needs.
* Adventist Private School is a religious, private and subsidized school owned by Seventh-day Adventist Church Corporation and teach from pre-school to primary school.[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* The British School Patagonia is part of Red de colegios Patagonia and a private and subsidized school, from pre-school to secondary school
* Pumahue School, member of [Cognita School](/wiki/Cognita "Cognita") is a private, non-subsidized school, from pre-school to secondary school.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* Salesiano Padre Jose Fernández Perez, is a Catholic, private and subsided school, that ranging from pre-school to secondary school with support to children with special needs, as well as technical and adults school.[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* Deutsche Schule (German Institute) of Puerto Montt is a private, non-subsidized school member of DS CHILE "Association of German schools in Chile" with an international exchange and support from Germany, teaching from pre-school to secondary school.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* Industrial Lyceum of Puerto Montt is a public, secondary school with a focus on technical professions.
* Lafquen Montessori School, Known to use [Montessori method](/wiki/Montessori_education "Montessori education") is a private and subsidized school, from pre-school to secondary-school with support to children with special needs.[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* Mother Isabel Larragaña Adult school is a Catholic and Private school with a primary and secondary school program for adults.[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* Joaquin de los Andes Auditory and Language Special School is an [holistic](/wiki/Holism "Holism"), private and subsidized school for pre-school children with [SLI](/wiki/Specific_language_impairment "Specific language impairment") and Deafness.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Universities

* [Universidad de Los Lagos](/wiki/Universidad_de_Los_Lagos "Universidad de Los Lagos"), Campus Puerto Montt (Chinquihue)[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* [Universidad Austral de Chile](/wiki/Universidad_Austral_de_Chile "Universidad Austral de Chile") (UACh), Campus Puerto Montt[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* [Universidad San Sebastián](/wiki/Universidad_San_Sebasti%C3%A1n "Universidad San Sebastián"), Campus Patagonia[[35]](#cite_note-35)
* Universidad Santo Tomás, Campus Puerto Montt[[36]](#cite_note-36)
